edible_parts: Flowers (cooked) and fruit (cooked); flower stalk (roasted, historic).
other_uses: Evergreen rosette of stiff sword leaves edged with curly fibers, sending up tall spikes of creamy bell flowers.
prep: The flowers and young fruit are eaten cooked and the emerging flower stalk roasted; the tough leaf fibers make cordage. Grown as a bold architectural evergreen for dry sites.
risks: Leaf tips are sharp; roots contain saponins (not eaten).
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
